changes.value:
## What

Implement `get_references` — find all references to a symbol. More complex than definition/hover because it groups results by file and enriches each reference with its enclosing symbol.

### `get_references` — `textDocument/references`
- New file: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/get_references.rs`
- Takes `LspContext` + `GetReferencesOptions { file_path, line, character, include_declaration, max_results }`
- Calls `textDocument/didOpen`, `textDocument/references` (with `context.includeDeclaration`), `textDocument/didClose`
- Parses response as array of locations
- Truncates to `max_results` (default 100)
- Groups references by file into `FileReferenceGroup` for readability
- **Key enrichment**: for each reference location, queries `lsp_symbols` to find the enclosing function/method — answers \"what function references this?\" not just \"where is it referenced?\"

### Result types
```rust
pub struct ReferencesResult {
    pub references: Vec<ReferenceLocation>,
    pub total_count: usize,
    pub by_file: Vec<FileReferenceGroup>,
}
pub struct ReferenceLocation {
    pub file_path: String,
    pub range: LspRange,
    pub containing_symbol: Option<String>,  // enclosing function from index
}
pub struct FileReferenceGroup {
    pub file_path: String,
    pub count: usize,
    pub references: Vec<ReferenceLocation>,
}
```

### Files to create/modify
- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/get_references.rs` — new
- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/mod.rs` — re-export
- `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/lib.rs` — re-export
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` — registration + handler

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns all references grouped by file
- [ ] Each reference includes the name of its enclosing symbol (when available in the index)
- [ ] Respects `max_results` limit, reports `total_count` regardless
- [ ] `include_declaration` parameter controls whether the declaration itself appears
- [ ] Appears as MCP operation under `code_context` tool

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: grouping logic correctly buckets references by file
- [ ] Unit test: `max_results` truncation works while preserving `total_count`
- [ ] Unit test: enclosing symbol lookup finds the tightest-fitting `lsp_symbols` row containing the range
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-code-context` passes

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

#lsp-live

 ## What

-Implement `get_references` — find all references to a symbol. More complex than definition/hover because it groups results by file and enriches each reference with its enclosing symbol.
+Implement `get_references` — find all references to a symbol. Uses layered resolution with grouping and enclosing-symbol enrichment.

-### `get_references` — `textDocument/references`
+### `get_references` — layered
 - New file: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/get_references.rs`
-- Takes `LspContext` + `GetReferencesOptions { file_path, line, character, include_declaration, max_results }`
-- Calls `textDocument/didOpen`, `textDocument/references` (with `context.includeDeclaration`), `textDocument/didClose`
-- Parses response as array of locations
-- Truncates to `max_results` (default 100)
-- Groups references by file into `FileReferenceGroup` for readability
-- **Key enrichment**: for each reference location, queries `lsp_symbols` to find the enclosing function/method — answers \"what function references this?\" not just \"where is it referenced?\"
+- Takes `LayeredContext` + `GetReferencesOptions { file_path, line, character, include_declaration, max_results }`
+- **Layer 1 (live LSP)**: `didOpen` → `textDocument/references` → `didClose`. Full cross-file reference finding.
+- **Layer 2 (LSP index)**: Query `lsp_call_edges` where the symbol appears as callee — gives callers as a proxy for references. Less complete than live references but covers call sites.
+- **Layer 3 (tree-sitter)**: Text search for the symbol name across `ts_chunks`. Noisy (catches comments, strings) but provides something.
+- **Enrichment**: For each reference location, use `enrich_location()` to find the enclosing function — answers "what function references this?" not just "where?"
+- Groups results by file into `FileReferenceGroup`

 }
 pub struct ReferenceLocation {
     pub file_path: String,
     pub range: LspRange,
-    pub containing_symbol: Option<String>,  // enclosing function from index
+    pub containing_symbol: Option<String>,
 }
 pub struct FileReferenceGroup {
     pub file_path: String,

-- [ ] Appears as MCP operation under `code_context` tool
+- [ ] Returns live references when LSP daemon running
+- [ ] Falls back to call-edge based references from LSP index
+- [ ] Falls back to text search from tree-sitter as last resort
+- [ ] Each reference enriched with enclosing symbol name

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Unit test: `max_results` truncation works while preserving `total_count`
-- [ ] Unit test: enclosing symbol lookup finds the tightest-fitting `lsp_symbols` row containing the range

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,14 +1,14 @@
 ## What

-Implement `get_references` — find all references to a symbol. Uses layered resolution with grouping and enclosing-symbol enrichment.
+Implement `get_references` — find all references to a symbol. Uses layered resolution via `LayeredContext` with grouping and enclosing-symbol enrichment.

 ### `get_references` — layered
 - New file: `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/get_references.rs`
-- Takes `LayeredContext` + `GetReferencesOptions { file_path, line, character, include_declaration, max_results }`
-- **Layer 1 (live LSP)**: `didOpen` → `textDocument/references` → `didClose`. Full cross-file reference finding.
-- **Layer 2 (LSP index)**: Query `lsp_call_edges` where the symbol appears as callee — gives callers as a proxy for references. Less complete than live references but covers call sites.
-- **Layer 3 (tree-sitter)**: Text search for the symbol name across `ts_chunks`. Noisy (catches comments, strings) but provides something.
-- **Enrichment**: For each reference location, use `enrich_location()` to find the enclosing function — answers "what function references this?" not just "where?"
+- Takes `&LayeredContext` + `GetReferencesOptions { file_path, line, character, include_declaration, max_results }`
+- **Layer 1 (live LSP)**: `ctx.lsp_notify()` didOpen → `ctx.lsp_request("textDocument/references", ...)` → `ctx.lsp_notify()` didClose. Full cross-file reference finding.
+- **Layer 2 (LSP index)**: `ctx.lsp_callers_of(symbol_id)` — reverse call edges as a proxy for references. Covers call sites but misses type references, field accesses, etc.
+- **Layer 3 (tree-sitter)**: `ctx.ts_chunks_matching(symbol_name, max)` — text search for the symbol name across chunks. Noisy (comments, strings) but provides something.
+- **Enrichment**: For each reference location, `ctx.enrich_location()` to find the enclosing function — answers "what function references this?" not just "where?"
 - Groups results by file into `FileReferenceGroup`

 ### Result types
@@ -17,17 +17,7 @@
     pub references: Vec<ReferenceLocation>,
     pub total_count: usize,
     pub by_file: Vec<FileReferenceGroup>,
-    pub source_layer: String,
-}
-pub struct ReferenceLocation {
-    pub file_path: String,
-    pub range: LspRange,
-    pub containing_symbol: Option<String>,
-}
-pub struct FileReferenceGroup {
-    pub file_path: String,
-    pub count: usize,
-    pub references: Vec<ReferenceLocation>,
+    pub source_layer: SourceLayer,
 }
 ```

@@ -38,18 +28,17 @@
 - `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/code_context/mod.rs` — registration + handler

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Returns live references when LSP daemon running
-- [ ] Falls back to call-edge based references from LSP index
-- [ ] Falls back to text search from tree-sitter as last resort
-- [ ] Each reference enriched with enclosing symbol name
+- [ ] Returns live references via `ctx.lsp_request()` when available
+- [ ] Falls back to `ctx.lsp_callers_of()` for call-edge based references
+- [ ] Falls back to `ctx.ts_chunks_matching()` for text search
+- [ ] Each reference enriched with enclosing symbol via `ctx.enrich_location()`
 - [ ] Grouped by file; respects `max_results`; reports `total_count`
-- [ ] `source_layer` indicates which layer provided results

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Unit test: grouping logic buckets correctly by file
 - [ ] Unit test: `max_results` truncation preserves `total_count`
-- [ ] Unit test: enclosing symbol lookup finds tightest-fitting symbol
-- [ ] Unit test: fallback to call-edge references
+- [ ] Unit test: enclosing symbol enrichment via `enrich_location()`
+- [ ] Unit test: fallback to `lsp_callers_of()` returns call-site references
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-code-context` passes

 ## Workflow
